figures.caption: Table 3. Per-model scores: WMF-AM Score, Outcome Correctness (OC), Agent Battery Score (ABS), and Yoked control accuracy (N=20, 13 families). WMF-AM Score = 4-seed mean at K ∈ {3, 5, 7}; OC = 100- item general-knowledge battery (Outcome Correctness; baseline predictor); ABS = 10-task deterministic agent battery (downstream criterion; pre-specified primary analysis). WMF-AM (pre-specified primary): τ=0.612 (p<0.001, confirmatory, C). OC → ABS: τ=0.428 (p=0.012, exploratory, E). Yoked column: cancellationcontrol accuracy (original 15 models only).
Table 5. Comparison to Minerva Quantity State. WMF-AM does not claim benchmark superiority; it contributes three additions to the existing arithmetic state-tracking paradigm.

Table 6. K-sweep: discriminability vs. step count. τ=τ(probe,Agent); N = models with per-K data. K=3–7: WMF-AM per-depth means (N=12 models for which per-K breakdowns were collected; the remaining 8 were tested only at the composite level); K=20: 5-model inline test; K=200: full 20-model head-to-head.
Table 11. Glossary of key terms and acronyms.

Table 4. Predictor comparison: WMF-AM Score vs. alternative predictors of ABS. C = pre-specified; E = exploratory. ∆τ (WMF vs. Yoked): 0.491 (p=0.022, bootstrap, inferentially confirmed). ∆τ (WMF vs. OC/params): directional but not confirmed (95% CI overlaps zero).
Figure 2. WMF-AM accuracy by depth K (N=15, chat template). deepseek-r1:14b (red) maintains nearperfect accuracy; all others degrade sharply at K ≥ 5. Top 5 models highlighted.

Table 9. Development status of all 11 CEF sub-dimensions. Status key A: robust differentiation + multi-seed reliability; B: differentiation shown, limited seeds/models; C: probe redesigned, initial results; D: ceiling/floor effect, needs redesign; E: protocol specified, no pilot data.

Table 10. CEF at a glance. Each sub-dimension’s task type, scoring rule, and pilot status. Tier 1 = novel construct; Tier 2 = novel reframing. Pilot: ✓= pilot-supported, ∼= preliminary, — = protocol only.
Table 16. Novelty tier for all 11 sub-dimensions. Tier 1 = novel; Tier 2 = novel reframing.

Figure 4. Validity evidence heatmap. Convergent and divergent correlations across CEF probes and external benchmarks (N=15). WMF-AM shows high divergent validity (low correlation with completion) and moderate convergent validity with agent battery scores.
Table 8. Representative benchmarks and evaluation work, grouped by measurement type and CEF gap addressed.

Table 1. Study map: complete analysis register. All τ = Kendall’s τ -b. C = pre-specified primary (Bonferronicorrected at α=0.025); E = exploratory (hypothesis-generating only; require held-out replication before drawing strong conclusions). Predictor: WMF-AM Score; Criterion: Agent Battery Score (ABS); Baseline: Outcome Correctness (OC).
Table 15. MCC-CE-v2 (N=8). Error rates 23–40% (floor eliminated); flagging near zero for all models.
Table 14. EMC-lite (N=7, hardened protocol). Source attribution shows strongest differentiation (spread = 0.500).
Table 7. CEF dimensions: target, failure signature, and exclusions. Detailed sub-dimension definitions appear in Appendix H.

Figure 3. CEF taxonomy. Four proposed dimensions (WMF, MCC, EMC, CLA) with sub-dimensions, cognitive anchors, and probe mappings. Only WMF-AM is empirically validated in this paper; other dimensions are included as design context.

Figure 1. (a) Completion clusters while WMF-AM spans a wide range (N=20, 13 families). (b) WMF-AM predicts downstream agent performance (τ=0.612, p<0.001; pre-specified confirmatory). Exploratory: partial τ=0.411 (p=0.011) after controlling for completion (same sample; requires held-out replication). Blue circles = original 7; orange squares = expansion 8; green triangles = small models (0.5B–2B).
Table 13. Study matrix: all experiments, models, and sample sizes. †llama3.1:70b added for MCC-CE-v2 only.
